title: Bellucia wurdackiana
taxonomy: Bellucia wurdackiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is native to the tropical rainforests of South America, specifically in countries such as Brazil and Peru. The plant is a member of the order Myrtales and is closely related to other Melastomataceae species. At the family level, it belongs to the subfamily Bellucioideae. Its classification also includes the genus Bellucia, which is unique to this particular species.
uses: Bellucia wurdackiana is a plant with reported medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. It is said to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various health issues.
